action.hypothesis: Enabling block-Hadamard preprocessing on the current strongest K=16 family may improve the small-K tradeoff by decorrelating activations before routing/codebook selection, without opening another architecture branch.
action.summary: Severe stagnation policy and operator hints both argue against more architecture churn. `USE_HADAMARD` is already implemented, appears untested in prior rounds, and is a cleaner preprocessing probe than introducing new code paths. I am targeting the existing K=16 frontier family to maximize the chance of a non-dominated smaller-K point under the new fixed EF=16 regime.
action.self_review: This is a disciplined single-variable preprocessing test on an existing frontier family. The main risk is regime drift because operator policy now fixes EF=16, so results will not be directly apples-to-apples with older EF=12 anchors; however, establishing whether Hadamard helps in the new regime is higher value than another minor architecture variant.
notes_to_memory: Round 122 shifts from architecture search to preprocessing per operator guidance: first explicit `USE_HADAMARD=1` probe on an existing frontier family.
Target K=16 because smaller K remains the highest-value regime and routed two-stage soft-codebook is the strongest available backbone there.
Keep the override set minimal and exact so attribution stays on preprocessing rather than architecture or optimizer drift.
next_hypotheses: If Hadamard improves the K=16 point, propagate it to K=32 on the same family to test whether the gain is localized to the small-K lane or general.
If Hadamard is neutral or worse here, keep preprocessing skepticism high and try the next under-explored training dimension: `OPTIMIZER=adam` on an existing competitive K=16 or K32 family.
If the EF=16 regime itself looks unstable relative to prior EF=12 behavior, run one plain non-Hadamard EF=16 control on the same family before drawing strong conclusions about preprocessing.

I am targeting the existing K=16 frontier family to maximize the chance of a non-dominated smaller-K point under the new fixed EF=16 regime.
